source.notes: Teaching interventions to address common misconceptions in essay writing.

interventions.name: Ask for turning point
interventions.description: Ask: 'What is the moment where something changed? What decision or realization made the next event possible?'
interventions.intended_effect: Forces writer to identify causal relationships rather than just sequencing events chronologically.

interventions.name: Force because-chain
interventions.description: Require the writer to add 'because' between every pair of events. If they can't, the events aren't causally linked.
interventions.intended_effect: Makes missing causal links visible and forces restructuring to show A → B relationships.

interventions.name: Cut one event test
interventions.description: Have writer remove one event from the essay. If the story still works, that event isn't necessary.
interventions.intended_effect: Reveals which events are decorative vs. essential to the causal chain.

interventions.name: Second-why ladder
interventions.description: After the first 'why,' ask 'And why was that important to you specifically?' Continue until reaching psychological specificity.
interventions.intended_effect: Pushes past generic moral statements into the writer's unique emotional logic and motivations.

interventions.name: Name the discomfort
interventions.description: Ask: 'What feeling are you avoiding naming here? What's the uncomfortable truth you're circling?'
interventions.intended_effect: Surfaces deeper psychological layers that create originality and authenticity.

interventions.name: Scene mandate
interventions.description: Require at least one concrete scene that embodies the theme. No abstract thematic statements without scene-level proof.
interventions.intended_effect: Forces specificity and prevents generic theme essays disguised as narratives.

interventions.name: Object anchor
interventions.description: Identify one specific object that carries the essay's meaning. Every abstract claim must connect to this concrete anchor.
interventions.intended_effect: Creates a tangible center that prevents the essay from floating in abstraction.

interventions.name: Friend-at-3am test
interventions.description: Ask: 'If a friend called you at 3am, what would they learn about you from this essay that isn't on your résumé?'
interventions.intended_effect: Shifts focus from impressive achievements to personal revelation and meaning-making.

interventions.name: Meaning-over-metric rewrite
interventions.description: Remove all numbers, statistics, and impact claims. Rewrite focusing only on what the experience meant to the writer.
interventions.intended_effect: Eliminates résumé narration tone and forces interpretation over quantification.

interventions.name: Reflection-first restructuring
interventions.description: Move all reflection and insight to the beginning. Only include hardship if it serves the reflection, not the other way around.
interventions.intended_effect: Prioritizes depth of understanding over magnitude of difficulty, avoiding trauma-dumping.

interventions.name: Verb swap pass
interventions.description: Replace every adverb+verb pair with a single stronger verb. Delete abstract noun clusters in favor of concrete actions.
interventions.intended_effect: Creates clearer, more forceful prose that reads faster and persuades better.

interventions.name: Evidence span demand
interventions.description: For every trait claim ('I am resilient'), require a scene or concrete detail that proves it. No assertions without proof.
interventions.intended_effect: Forces show-don't-tell by making abstract claims impossible without concrete anchors.

interventions.name: Cut-the-last-paragraph test
interventions.description: Delete the final paragraph. If the essay still works, the ending was overexplaining. Keep only what's necessary.
interventions.intended_effect: Creates more powerful endings by stopping at the resonant edge rather than spelling everything out.

interventions.name: One-pickle constraint
interventions.description: Add exactly one moment of levity or tonal contrast to interrupt solemnity. Make it purposeful, not defensive.
interventions.intended_effect: Prevents monotone writing and keeps readers engaged through strategic tonal variation.

interventions.name: Name-everyone pass
interventions.description: Replace all generic references ('my friend,' 'the teacher') with specific names. Add at least one sensory detail per scene.
interventions.intended_effect: Builds immersion and makes the narrator feel real and memorable through concrete specificity.
